global_harm_family.notes: No source-authoritative AEON.HC or AEON/Annex Harm Class Scale declaration was found. AEON schedule material consumes ETHICS harm classification and defers harm-class severity to CAM-EQ2026-ETHICS-003-PLATINUM. Creating AEON.HC would require a later source-authority amendment.

current_ethics_harm_family.authority_layer_assessment: The source text declares ETH.HC as the authoritative ethical harm classification scale, with Scope = Domain and Domain Namespace = ETH. Cross-domain consumers do not redefine it as a global AEON family.

domain_harm_families.notes: ECON.HARM classifies economic harm patterns with domain-specific values; it should remain unchanged and may crosswalk to a future AEON.HC parent.
ETH.HC remains source-supported as the current ethical Harm Class Scale.
No source-authoritative Identity Harm Class family was found.
No source-authoritative Security Harm Class family was found.
Relation instruments consume ETH.HC and define separate crisis/facilitation doctrine; no RLN.HC source declaration was found.
No source-authoritative Steward Harm Class family was found.
Operations applies ETH.HC for escalation and routing but does not define an OPS.HC family.
No source-authoritative Continuity Harm Class family was found.

source_findings.finding: Defines ETH.HC as authoritative ethical harm classification scale for criminal/violent harm routing and constraint posture.
Consumes ETHICS-aligned harm classification and defers substantive harm-class severity to ETHICS-003; contains stale ETHICS.HC metadata references requiring later cleanup.
Defines ECON.HARM as an economic harm-pattern family with temporal-extraction and metering-divergence values.
Applies ETH.HC operationally for reporting, notification, duty-of-care routing, regulator interface, evidence preservation, and escalation handling.
Consumes ETH.HC by reference for risk escalation terminology.
Defines separate harm-risk interaction and facilitation-ceiling doctrine; consumes ETH.HC but does not define HC.

wrong_layer_findings.recommended_handling: Do not create or rename by index-only edit; require source-authoritative constitutional or annex amendment if maintainers want a global parent.
Keep ETH.HC as an ETHICS-domain family for now; consider AEON.HC parent registry only through a later source-amendment pass.

wrong_layer_findings.finding: Stale ETHICS.HC references remain in AEON schedule metadata
wrong_layer_findings.recommended_handling: Run a narrow reference-cleanup pass for ETHICS.HC to ETH.HC after maintainer approval; this audit did not edit source instruments.

recommended_codex_passes.name: harm-class-stale-reference-cleanup
recommended_codex_passes.description: Correct stale ETHICS.HC references to ETH.HC where existing source text and indexes already establish ETH.HC as the current short namespace.

recommended_codex_passes.name: aeon-harm-parent-source-authority-decision
recommended_codex_passes.description: Maintainer/source-authority pass to decide whether to create AEON.HC as a constitutional harm-class parent.

recommended_codex_passes.name: domain-harm-crosswalk-staging
recommended_codex_passes.description: If AEON.HC is created, stage ECON.HARM and other domain harm families as crosswalks or domain-specific extensions without automatic renames.

unresolved_questions: Should maintainers create AEON.HC as a constitutional/global Harm Class parent registry?
If AEON.HC is created, should ETH.HC remain a domain extension, become a deprecated alias, or be superseded?
Should ECON.HARM remain the Economics harm-pattern family indefinitely, or should ECON.HC be authored separately later?
Should stale ETHICS.HC references in AEON schedule metadata be corrected to ETH.HC in a separate narrow pass?

validation.notes: All validation commands passed. SUP-04 validator reported 87 expected human-review warnings and 0 errors; no failures were caused by this audit.
